source_intake_record.raw_question_text: analyze the RL circuit of figure. What is \( V_\text{R} \) in the limits \( \omega \to 0 \)?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the figure'
the needed circuit topology/component arrangement is not specified in text alone
multi_step: requires identifying the RL circuit configuration from the figure
requires applying limit behavior as omega -> 0 for circuit analysis
answering likely combines visual circuit interpretation with physics/electrical reasoning
multiple-choice format lowers open-ended complexity somewhat
verifiability: answer_present=true with a specific symbolic target: 'V_R -> E_0'
choices include an exact matching option (C)
answer_type=short_text is easy to compare against the expected expression
the result is governed by standard RL low-frequency behavior, making rule-based checking feasible

risk_flags: circuit topology is image-dependent and not recoverable from question text alone
symbolic notation matching may need normalization (e.g. E_0 vs equivalent formatting)

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和关键图像资产均存在，asset registry 已通过，核心语义完整可用
该题虽强依赖图像中的电路拓扑，但图像文件已完整提供，因此不构成候选池准入缺口
可验证性高，答案为明确的符号结果，便于后续标准化比对
仅存在符号归一化等轻微处理风险，不足以降低为 low_priority

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: Analyze the RL circuit shown in the figure. What is \( V_\text{R} \) in the limit \( \omega \to 0 \)?

force_requires_image=true, so requires_image remains true and cleaning_path is multimodal_full.
